introMessage: Contract disputes can feel overwhelming. Let's work out where you stand and what your options are.

questions.helpText: Pick the one that fits closest.

options.label: Someone broke the terms of our agreement
Someone won't pay what they owe me
I paid for a service that wasn't delivered properly
I bought goods that were faulty or not as described
I need a contract reviewed before signing
Someone ended our contract unfairly
I'm a content creator with a brand, agency, or platform dispute
Something else

questions.text: What best describes your creator dispute?
questions.helpText: This helps us tailor the guidance to your specific situation.

options.label: A brand or sponsor hasn't paid me for content I created
A brand used my content beyond what we agreed
My management or agency isn't honouring our agreement
A platform removed my content or restricted my account
A collaboration partner didn't deliver on their side
Someone used my content without permission

questions.helpText: If it's ongoing, enter when the problem first started.
Written contracts are easier to enforce, but verbal agreements can also be binding.

options.label: Yes, there's a written contract
Some things were written, some were verbal
It was a verbal agreement only
I'm not sure

questions.text: Roughly how much is at stake?
questions.helpText: This helps us advise on the right course of action. Claims under £10,000 can go to the Small Claims Court.

options.label: Under £1,000
I'm not sure

questions.text: Have you raised this with the other party?
questions.helpText: Courts expect you to try to resolve the issue directly before taking legal action.

options.label: Yes, in writing
Yes, but only verbally
No, not yet
I tried but they won't respond

questions.helpText: Evidence is key in contract disputes. Select all that apply.

options.label: The contract or agreement
Emails, letters, or messages about the dispute
Invoices or payment records
Receipts for losses or expenses
Photos of faulty goods or work
Witnesses
I don't have any yet

questions.text: Have you tried mediation or alternative dispute resolution?
questions.helpText: Courts prefer it if you've attempted to resolve things without going to court. Mediation is often free or low cost.

options.label: Yes, we tried but it didn't work
No, not yet
What's mediation?

questions.text: What outcome are you looking for?

options.label: I want to be paid what I'm owed
I want compensation for my losses
I want the agreement to be honoured
I want to get out of the contract
I just want to understand my options

strong.message: The facts described suggest there may be grounds for a claim. Here is what to do next.

maybe.message: There may be a claim here. The strength will depend on the evidence and whether the breach can be demonstrated.

difficult.message: This may be harder to pursue on current evidence, but steps can still be taken. Here is where to start.
